etymology.description: 亡(亡) (wáng, "lose") as phonetic + 心(心) (xīn, "heart") as semantic = to lose something in the heart -> forget
etymology.interpretation: The character 忘(wàng, "forget") combines the phonetic element 亡(亡) (wáng, "lose") with 心(心) (xīn, "heart"), indicating the concept of losing something from the heart, hence the idea of forgetting.

examples.chinese: 他总是忘记带钥匙(他總是忘記帶鑰匙)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì wàngjì dài yàoshi.
examples.english: He always forgets to bring the keys.

examples.chinese: 请不要忘记你的承诺(請不要忘記你的承諾)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bùyào wàngjì nǐ de chéngnuò.

examples.chinese: 有时我会忘记时间(有時我會忘記時間)，错过重要的事情(錯過重要的事情)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒushí wǒ huì wàngjì shíjiān, cuòguò zhòngyào de shìqíng.
examples.english: Sometimes I forget the time and miss important things.
